title: PROTECT Student Athletes Act
plain_summary: This bill makes it against the law for betting companies to take bets on how well individual college athletes perform in their games. People can still bet on which team wins or loses, but they cannot bet on things like how many points one player scores. The Federal Trade Commission will watch to make sure betting companies follow this rule. If a company breaks this law, they will get in trouble and have to pay penalties.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Providing Responsible Oversight and Transparency and Ensuring Collegiate Trust for Student Athletes Act or the PROTECT Student Athletes Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. Sports betting, including specific bets about how individual student athletes perform, makes up a big part of business between states. Sports betting happens across state lines through online websites and physical betting locations, with people from many different states participating. The money made from sports betting on college sports affects the whole country's economy, including money from advertisements, TV broadcasting rights, and other business activities connected to college sports. Making sure student athletes are safe and protected is important to the whole nation. Rules about betting, especially bets about how student athletes perform, are needed to protect the honesty, health, and school environments of these athletes. The federal government has the power under the Commerce Clause of the Constitution to control business between states. Since sports betting happens between states, federal rules are needed to keep the same standards everywhere and protect student athletes.

sections.title: Section 3 Prohibition on Prop Bets Involving Student Athletes
sections.text: No person or business that runs betting or gambling may accept any bet that includes a covered prop bet. A covered prop bet means a bet on whether something will or will not happen during a college sports game, specifically about what a student athlete does or achieves. This action or achievement may or may not directly affect who wins the game. The Federal Trade Commission will enforce this rule. If someone breaks this rule, it will be treated the same as breaking a rule about unfair or deceptive business practices under the Federal Trade Commission Act. The Federal Trade Commission will enforce this section using the same methods, powers, and authority it has under the Federal Trade Commission Act. Anyone who breaks this rule will face the same penalties and have the same rights as provided in the Federal Trade Commission Act. An intercollegiate sporting event means a sports game played at the college level where a national organization that promotes or controls college sports sets the rules for which students can participate. A student athlete means any person who plays in college sports games.
